mnemonics.title: Binary Tree Definition
mnemonics.content: B.A.T. - Binary tree: Each node has At most Two children (left and right)

mnemonics.title: Height vs Depth
mnemonics.content: Height goes DOWN from root (leaves), Depth goes UP to root (root = 0)

mnemonics.title: Max Nodes Formula
mnemonics.content: 2^(h+1) - 1: Think '2 Hats Plus 1 Minus' - 2 to the power of height+1, minus 1 nodes maximum

mnemonics.title: Leaf Node Property
mnemonics.content: Leaf nodes = Internal nodes + 1 (for non-empty trees)

mnemonics.title: Level Node Count
mnemonics.content: Level 0 has 1 node, Level 1 has 2, Level 2 has 4 - powers of 2 rule!

acronyms.usage: Short form when writing code or taking notes
Traversal order: visit Left subtree, then Node, then Right subtree
Visit Node first, then Left subtree, then Right subtree
Visit Left subtree, then Right subtree, then Node

visualTips.visualization: Draw as upside-down tree: root at top, branches going down, left child on left branch, right child on right branch - like a triangle shape
Count edges (lines), not nodes. Root to leaf path: each edge = +1 height. Single node tree has height 0.
Fill levels left-to-right, no gaps. Like filling a pyramid from left, every level must be full before starting next.
Every node has either 0 or 2 children - no partial nodes allowed!
All levels completely filled AND all leaf nodes at same level - looks like a perfect triangle

analogies.analogy: Think of a family tree where each person can have at most 2 children (left=firstborn, right=second-born)
CEO at top of company hierarchy - all decisions flow downward from single leader
End of branches on a real tree - nothing grows further from them
Each node is a parent with up to 2 kids - like a nuclear family with maximum 2 children
Reading a book: Preorder = title first, Inorder = page order, Postorder = appendices first
